metadata.title:
SPOKANE MEN WILL


OPERATE old SHELTER

pages.raw_text:
The machinery when the first pro-
jcCt was nearly completed was put
on the ground but has never been
set.


MIR. Robertson, after investigation,
realized the possibilities of the
plant, and as soon as practicable
set about interesting others in the
starting of the smelter.


The capacity of the works will be
considerably over 100 tons daily
and the property is now in the
hands of the three gentlemen
named.


DR., Van Osdel is now in Oakland,
Cal, assisting in the planning of a
new Baptist church there, and Mr..
Robertson is visiting in the Palouse
country. Mr.. Van Osdcl refused to
publish the details.


Talks with mining men in the
city this morning brought out
many opinions favorable to the en-
terprise. One of its important re-
suits would be the active opening
up of all workings contiguous to
the city which are now lying dor
mant.
